title:
Module 5
Phone Setup / Warmup
subtitle: Social Media Setup for Your Phone and Warmup for Your Social Media Accounts

blocks.content: Your device setup is the foundation of account safety. Platforms track device IDs, IP addresses, and usage patterns. If you run multiple AI personas from the same phone on the same network, you are asking to get flagged. This lesson covers the correct way to set up your devices before creating a single account.

items: One dedicated phone per persona (ideal)
If sharing a device: max 2 accounts per phone
Use separate browser profiles or app clones if needed
Never log into two personas on the same app simultaneously
Factory reset any used phone before starting a new persona
Use mobile data (4G/5G) during warmup — not WiFi
If using WiFi: use a VPN set to a US location
Each persona should ideally have a different IP footprint
Never use public WiFi for account activity
Each account needs a unique phone number for verification
Prepaid SIM cards work perfectly
Virtual numbers (Google Voice, etc.) are riskier but usable
Never share a phone number across multiple personas
Instagram
Threads (linked to Instagram)
TikTok
Telegram
VPN app (if needed)
Photo gallery app for content storage

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Phone setup checklist graphic showing device, SIM, VPN, and app configuration

blocks.content: Account creation is not just signing up. Every decision you make in the first 24 hours sends signals to the platform's algorithm. This lesson covers how to create accounts that look human from the very first second.

items: Human-sounding name — first name or first name + initial
No random numbers (e.g., emma38472)
No sexual or suggestive words
Easy to remember and search
Same username across all platforms for this persona

left.content:
Username: emma.laine / lilyy.rose / sofiamrr
Real-sounding, memorable, no red flags.

Username: hotgirl_2024 / xXsexyEmmaXx / model.promo.page
Instant red flags for platforms and audiences.

items: Add a profile photo (clear face shot of your AI model)
Write a short, casual bio — personality-driven, not salesy
No links in bio during warmup — add after 3+ weeks
No selling language, no emojis spam
Set account to public

right.content:
Bio: '22 ☀️ dreaming in california'
Simple, human, personality-driven.

Bio: '🔥 DM for exclusive content 🔥 Link below 👇'
Screams promotional account. Will get flagged.

blocks.title: TikTok Exception
blocks.content: On TikTok, do NOT add username, bio, or profile picture immediately after account creation. Close the app for 20 minutes after creating the account. Then come back and add details slowly over the first 2-3 days.

The warmup phase is the most important phase of your entire account lifecycle. It exists to teach the algorithm that your account is a real human. If you rush this, everything after breaks. There are no shortcuts.
Duration: 21-28 days for Instagram/Threads. 9 days for TikTok. This phase is boring on purpose.

items: 2-3 carousels per week (face-focused, lifestyle, clean, SFW)
2-3 stories per week (selfies, food, random daily stuff)
No questions, no CTA, no suggestive captions
No links, no selling
Scroll for 15-30 minutes
Like posts from English-speaking creators
Comment occasionally (normal comments only)
Watch Reels and Stories naturally
Follow 5-10 accounts per day max
No posting, no bio changes
Scroll and watch content for 20-30 min/day
Like 10-15 videos per session
Follow 3-5 accounts per day
Add profile photo and bio
Post first piece of content (safe, simple)
Continue scrolling and engaging
Post 2-3 more pieces of content
Increase engagement slightly
Account is ready for regular posting

blocks.title: Critical Rule
blocks.content: If you rush the warmup, everything after breaks. There are no shortcuts. The platforms are watching your first 21 days more closely than any other period. Behave like a normal person, not a marketer.

sections.header: Lesson 4: Warmup Mistakes That Get You Banned

blocks.content: Most account bans happen during the warmup phase because people cannot resist skipping steps. This lesson covers every common mistake and why each one triggers platform flags.

items: Posting content on Day 1 (the platform has no trust in you yet)
Adding a link in bio during warmup (screams promotional account)
Following 50+ accounts per day (bot behavior)
Liking hundreds of posts in a session (automated pattern)
Using the same caption style on every post
Posting suggestive content before the account has trust
Using hashtags aggressively during warmup

left.title: The Invisible Flag
left.content: You will NOT get a warning. Platforms silently reduce your reach. You will not know you are shadowbanned until weeks later when nothing grows. Prevention is the only cure.

items: Wait 3-5 days before first post
Keep all warmup content clean and casual
Engage like a real user, not a marketer
Add link in bio only after week 3
Keep follow/unfollow activity low and natural
Post slowly and increase frequency gradually
Let the algorithm come to you — don't force it

right.content: Week 1: Scroll, like, follow 5 accounts. Week 2: First carousel post, 2 stories. Week 3: 3 carousels, daily stories. Week 4: Add link in bio, start Reels.

Day 1: 5 posts, 100 follows, link in bio, hashtag spam.
Result: Shadowban within 48 hours. Account is dead.

summary_points: One dedicated phone per persona — never share devices or IP addresses
Usernames must be human-sounding, memorable, and consistent across platforms
No links, no selling language, and no suggestive content during warmup
Instagram warmup takes 21-28 days — TikTok takes 9 days — no shortcuts
Behave like a real person: scroll, like, comment naturally before posting
Most bans happen during warmup because people rush — patience is mandatory
